reviews.review_description: Solid go-to game right here. Much recommend. I still play it over a year later. Building up those ridiculous guns until they downgrade the visuals doesn't get old. I'm certainly ready for some new barrels, caps, and canisters, though. Or better yet, Gunforge 2!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind review! :D

reviews.review_description: I've played VR for a good bit now, and this game is the first game I recommend to newcomers and it's the game I set friends up on to play for one of their first VR experiences. I really wish they'd either update the game or release a new version with more stuff... There's so much potential to add stuff to this masterpiece of a game.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind review. We are super grateful! :D

reviews.review_description: I’m currently level 15 and I finally beat the last boss of the game! This is officially my favorite game of my life! Such a great game to play after a long day of working hard on a farm! I’m 40 years old and I feel like a kid playing this game!

developer_response.body: oh, wow. Dang. That's really high praise. Thank you so much for playing our game and we're glad you enjoy it so much!

reviews.review_title: Tower of gun is the goat
reviews.review_description: Mothership Forge is a very good game too tower of guns. I hope you make like a vr version that can you move around like the actual game I really hope I wanna see all the bosses from Tower of guns in VR both of them my stars ￼ is five out of five ￼ and also why can I not get mother gunship on my console? ￼ I fully believe in you that you can make tower of guns ;)

developer_response.body: thanks so much for the kind review! Making Tower of Guns into a VR game would be pretty tricky, since it uses a much older core engine... however, it's good to know folks would be interested in playing that.. you are the first person to ever mention it! As for the console version of Mothergunship: it was taken down during a publisher change, but perhaps we will have updates about that in the coming year!

reviews.review_description: I really like the game my friend showed it to me when i first got my vr but i haven't talked to him in a bit but i just started playing this game agent and the memories are awsom i just wish more ppl would play this game

developer_response.body: Thanks friend! We were glad you're enjoying the game and the memories it's bringing back :-)

reviews.review_title: Best game for new vr players
reviews.review_description: So much fun the way you can costomise your run however you want and the thrill of being able to shoot lazer beams while doging kamakasing robot drones is amasing

developer_response.body: thank you! We worked hard to make the game as accessible as possible so new VR folks would enjoy it.

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this game. The crazy guns that you can make is so much fun. Each time you play it’s different. Doesn’t get boring. I just don’t understand why you can favorite a gun you made but can’t use it again. I am playing in co op.

developer_response.body: thank you so much for the kind works and and nice review! We greatly appreciate it. :)

reviews.review_description:
Guns, guns, guns, guns, and MORE guns. What else is there to say?

Insanely fun and awesome

developer_response.body: thanks! :-D

reviews.review_description: It could have been even better than the PC version, but it isn’t. Why? Because in reality, you can't move freely within the level. You’re limited to a small circle where you can barely move VERY slowly and try to either avoid enemies or dodge their shots. The environment, consequently, is small and non-interactive, no doors to choose, no alternative paths, just surviving until all the enemies in the level are done. The graphics are very nice, but since it’s a mobile device, it obviously doesn’t reach a PC gaming quality. The music is practically the same, or at least that’s how it seemed to me, and therefore very good. I won’t ask for a refund because it’s not bad anyway, but i recommend to those who don’t know it yet, the PC version, which, although not VR, remains the best, and from time to time they can find it discounted to half the price of this (discounted) VR version on GOG.
Not bad! Pretty fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much; we really appreciate the review! I hope you enjoy our work! :)

reviews.review_description: It's fun and I like thr graphics but I can't seem to build a gun on each hand?? My left trigger never fires even though I build a gun. And I can't see if all my guns on my right hand are firing as its only ever one bullet?

developer_response.body: Hm.. do you still see these issues? For the left hand, youll need a barrel on both hands to be able to fire.. so if that's set up and you still see a problem it might be related to your controllers (low battery or something?). For the right hand--you should be able to see all the bullets normally. If you HOLD to fire rather than clicking for each fire, do you see all the bullets? Let us know if you need more followup over on our discord (discord (dot) terribleposture (dot) com).

reviews.review_description: I love this game. Just wish you could start over with the guns you've saved as favorites. Easy to dive into and hours of fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're glad you liked our work!

reviews.review_description: I LOVED tower of guns back in the day. Easily 100+ hours and id find myself coming back to it every once in awhile. Saw this game and it reminded me of it.  INSTANT buy, not an ounce of regret. Would have even paid $40 or $50 with no regret. This game has a ridiculous amount of content, so much so that even playing through just three different biomes still feels like a new game everytime.  If you manage to unlock it all, you're still only Scratching the surface with all the combos. Was so happy to see your team is still thriving!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We're so glad you enjoyed the game. And yeah, hopefully you can see the Tower of Guns DNA still laced through forge. :-)

reviews.review_description: Cool

developer_response.body: Thank you!

reviews.review_description: very great game but one thing I don’t like about it. It takes forever for me to join the lobby with people.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the feedback and for the nice review. It's much appreciated!

reviews.review_description: Combine gun parts and make a ridiculous gun!! Fun game, esp if you have a little space to dodge irl

developer_response.body: Thanks! we're glad you liked our work!

reviews.review_description: What a blast! Came across this game during the vault sale and played the free trial. Instant buy! I've barely scraped the surface, maybe twenty attachments unlocked so far. Not everyone is a rogue like fan, but if you are, this is a must have for your library!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind review! :-D

reviews.review_title: i have made so many friends
reviews.review_description: tysm for this masterpiece but i do want just 2 more thing more ppl available in coop and more updates.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind review! :-)

reviews.review_description:
Barrels-

Death Ray: A powerful gun. Rarities: Legendary only. Info: A weapon with a reactor 
pointing forwards. It has four claw fingers that bend for its idle animation and spin while charging and firing. It has a cooldown of 10 seconds, and a charge-up of 5 seconds. It also has neutralize built in. It also cannot have its firerate increased AT ALL. Projectile: A massive (even larger with mega cap) red beam that lasts for 3 seconds and has a DPS of 1000. The beam can also be canceled, as it is required to hold down the trigger to keep the beam firing for the full 3 seconds.

Particle Accelerator: A gun with a unique projectile. Rarity: Rare or higher. Info: Has INFINITE bounce built into it. Cannot fire again until the projectile has hit the target or disappeared. Projectile: Two white orbs that spin around each other. They spin faster the longer they have existed and deal more damage the faster they spin. Eventually, they end up crashing into each other after existing for long enough (not when they hit an enemy though) and explode.

Soundwave Blaster: A gun that shoots sound. Rarities: Uncommon or higher. Info: Has piercing and deflect built in. Can stun enemies. Has a cooldown of 1.75 seconds. Projectile: A sound wave which is hard to see and is also quite fast. It also starts to deal less damage after existing for long enough.

Portal Gun: A gun that shoots portals. Rarities: Legendary only. Info: A weapon with a blue orb within a (possibly) glass jar connected to a spinning barrel. Deals no damage by itself, but quite useful if used correctly. When used, it shoots a portal. Any enemies or projectiles that get close to the portal get sucked into it, but not killed. When used again, it shoots another portal that shoots any projectiles or bombots absorbed by the first portal at nearby enemies, and launches any non-bombot enemies out once it hits something. Bounce does not affect the second portal, but can affect the first one. After shooting a second portal, it will shoot the first portal once the second hits something.

bobgun: the strongest weapon. Can only be used by devs or people given the ability to use it. Rarity: EXCLUSIVE. Info: Looks like a normal blaster, but when used, it shoots a ridiculous amount of Oblivigun bullets per second that deal 100000 damage each. It has the Mega cap’s ability built into it, because why not? It also has neutralize, four missile cap passives, 5 multishot passives (causing the ridiculous amount of bullets), jelly AND acid mines, and magnetize built into it. Has a rate of 2000. Like I said, the bobgun can only be used by developers or people given the ability to use it since it is this overpowered.

Tesla Coil: A Tesla coil. Rarities: Rare and above. Info: Has a small range but has a dps of 500. Has chain, neutralize, and multichain built into it, and fires all around its sides. Electrocutes any enemies hit with it. Quite useful when dealing with saw bots, staplehounds and bombots, but has a short range.

EMP launcher: A weapon with multiple lightning rods pointing forward from a generator. Rarity: Epic. Info: Shoots a ball of electricity that ONLY deals damage while in the air or when it contacts an enemy. The ball moves slowly, and zaps nearby enemies with electricity, dealing a measly 10 damage but stunning them. Has a unique ability built in: Disable. This ability disables projectiles that come near the ball, causing them to fall to the ground or (in the cause of the laser and blue energy ball) disappear. When the ball of electricity hits the ground or a wall, it emits an EMP that stuns nearby enemies, disables shields, and disables midair projectiles. The EMP deals no damage. If an enemy touches the ball, they get a ridiculous amount of electricity surging through them, causing them to overheat and, if it isn’t a boss, get their mechanical parts fried, killing them instantly. Has a firerate of 25.

Caps-

Discoverer’s cap: A legendary cap that looks like a magnifying glass. It gives 4 luck upon equipping it and also slightly increases the chance of getting an unlocked item that hasn’t been obtained yet while slightly decreasing the chance of getting obtained items (including itself).

Drill: A melee weapon that is common in the Mineshaft Zone (more on that later). Can deal damage by stabbing, but deals more damage if holding trigger where it starts drilling. Can instantly destroy force fields if needed.

Shielding Shield: A legendary shield that has 1 hit before it gets disabled. However, as weak as it sounds, it has a unique capability that makes it extremely powerful: it gains one extra maximum hit amount for each piece of armor you collect. Of course, I would add a weakness to something like this. Every hit amount you gain from armor can only be used once. Let’s say you have a max of 6 hits on a shielding shield. If your shield gets hit 5 times, the max goes back to one. It can have its maximum hit count increased by caps, but armor gives it a single use increase of maximum hits it can take.

the muncher: Yes, there are no capital letters in it’s name. A legendary melee weapon that looks similar to the Silver Teeth cap, but the teeth pointing forward. It deals damage when trigger is pressed. It deals 3500 damage per bite and has neutralize built in. Instantly destroys any forcefield it touches when trigger is pressed. Also gives one health when any type of Staplehound is killed with it.

The Final Straw cap: A legendary cap that looks like a black sphere on a pedestal. When you reach one health while you have a Final Straw cap, you produce a shockwave and become invincible while receiving buffs. The buffs are that you deal 5 times as much damage, your bullets move 10 times faster and each hit is a critical hit. However, after using the Final Straw cap, it destroys itself. Think that only one can be used at a time? WRONG. When you reach one health, ALL FINAL STRAW CAPS ARE ACTIVATED. Your buffs also get stronger for each final straw cap you have, with a maximum of 3 buffs per gun. Oh, I forgot to mention that the gun buffs only apply to the gun it is on, meaning that it won’t buff the gun on your right hand if it’s on your left hand, but you still get the invincibility.

Enemies-

Tankhound: A variant of the Staplehound that is larger and has much more health, having 8000 health, but it moves slower (taking 1.25 seconds to take one step) because of all the armor it wears, giving you more time to kill it. Appears during later areas in normal runs, and becomes more common the harder it gets in endless mode.

Speedy Bombot: A smaller bombot that moves in a zigzag and also is much faster, but stops moving when the player is in its range. It has one point of health and has a smaller explosion, but is still just as dangerous as a regular bombot. Appears after the first boss fight.

Staplehound Omega: Largest walking enemy on the list and in the game if it gets added. Huge Staplehound that is black in color and has a TON of health, having 15000 health. However, due to its size, it takes 3 seconds for it to take a single step. This means it takes longer for it to reach you, but if it does, it instantly kills you unless you have one piece of armor or the crystal ball cap. It also only appears on endless mode and on the secret zone, but it can only take routes where no ramps are required to reach you, as it cannot go up or down ramps. If the only way to reach you is a ramp, it cannot appear. It also can only appear once per level to not overwhelm players with lots of them.

Spike Launcher: A type of turret that shoots a spike. And I mean the same spikes that are shot by the final boss and by the secret boss. It only appears on the final boss’s zone, the secret zone, the Mineshaft Zone (more on that a teeny tiny bit later) and on endless mode. It has a bit more health than the giant red turret. It also has a large cooldown.

The Boomer: A large sphere with legs and a cannon. Has 10000 health in its first phase and 5000 in its second. In its first phase, it stands still and uses its cannon to shoot explosives at you. After its health reaches 0, it heals to 5000 health and starts walking towards the player while shooting you with its cannon. It also has a forcefield in its second phase. When it gets close enough to the player, it becomes invincible and explodes, killing both itself and the player. It’s self destruction also ignores armor and will kill the player unless they have the Crystal Ball cap. Only appears in the Secret zone and above. Can only appear once per three levels or so.

Screamers: Flying enemies that have 100 health. They will immediately head towards you the moment they spawn, and when they reach you, they scream right in your face. They deal no damage, but they apply temporary debuffs to you when they scream at you. They also die after they attack you once.

Splitters: Another type of flying enemy. They are quite large and shoot slow bullets that deal three points of damage to you. When killed, they split into two smaller, faster splitters that shoot faster bullets that deal 2 damage to you. When those splitters are killed, they split one final time into two even smaller and even faster splitters that shoot ridiculously fast bullets at you that deal 1 point of damage to you. The second splitters also have bullets that ignore deflect, and the final splitters have bullets that ignore neutralize AND deflect.

Flash Bombot: A normal looking and acting bombot. However, when it explodes, rather than dealing damage to you, it blinds you with whiteness for a few seconds, making you more vulnerable. While blinded, your guns are disabled. Also has white eyes instead of blue ones.

Mini Boomer: A smaller version of the Boomer enemy mentioned earlier. Shoots exploding cannonballs at you while running towards you. When it gets close, it explodes and deals three points of damage to you (including armor plates).k

Bosses and zones-

Mineshaft Zone: A dark zone where walking enemies, especially drillhounds (the walking enemies with a built in force field and drills) are quite common. The flying buzzsaw bots are also common here. Hard to see sometimes because of the darkness, but most of the time you can see. Like the secret zone with the pink spikes, this zone becomes available after defeating the final boss as an area unlockable with a key.

The Collector: The boss that comes at the end of the Mineshaft Zone. Can throw rocks at you, shoot spikes and lasers, and also launches drills at you. It also has hands that it uses to throw rocks and shoot drills from. Cannot take damage until it runs out of energy for a bit, where the four forcefield generators on its head become vulnerable. After one has been destroyed, it retreats to the underground for a bit while floating buzzsaw bots attack you, platforms come from the ground and drillhounds start attacking you. Once all enemies from this have been destroyed, the Collector comes back to fight again. After all four forcefield generators have been destroyed, the large power source bulb on the top of it’s head becomes vulnerable, and once this is destroyed, the Collector stops working and falls into the abyss of the chasm below before dying in a massive explosion.

The Storage Zone: Like the secret area with the pink spikes, all rooms here are challenge rooms. Comes after the Mineshaft Zone as an option along with Exit. Enemies here are quite hard. Gun part rooms and shops are more common than other rooms.

The Resource Guardian: Quite the unique boss. Comes at the end of the Storage Zone. Moves around much more than other bosses, as it will be climbing, running, hopping and dodging during the boss fight. Did I mention that the boss area is a flat floor with two piles of storage containers? Anyways, unlike other bosses, IT WILL ACTUALLY USE THE SAME GUN PARTS AND BARRELS THAT THE PLAYER CAN USE. It often makes powerful weapons with the gun parts it finds in the storage containers. It can also summon enemies as well. It also will not stop trying to fight the player, and gains no  I-frames during any part of the fight. Upon defeat, it will collapse to the ground and explode, with the rewards being gun parts and lots of purple shards.

Forge Zone: The forge itself. Comes at the end of the Storage zone as an option. Here, only Gun part rooms and Forge Upgrade rooms can spawn.

The Blacksmith: Quite possibly the hardest boss on this list. A large spherical mech piloted by its own little core. It also sports large claws attached to it and retractable weapons on the ship itself. It can teleport in random areas, fire missiles from it’s ship, shoot spikes faster than other bosses, shoot walls at you, shoot lasers with it’s claws, and shoot a HUGE laser from it’s core. To beat it, you destroy the claws first, then you destroy the core (which has a very, very, VERY large amount of health) where it will then malfunction and explode before falling to it’s destruction at the bottom of the forge, rewarding you with a big shard and three legendary gun parts to choose from. After both of its claws are destroyed, you must attack its core opening, which will temporarily open, exposing its core.

Gamemodes-

God Mode: Exactly what it sounds like. Obtained at 100000 (was thinking about 1 million but decided it was too overpriced) purple shards and once at least 150 (or more if you want) gun parts have been obtained. Makes the player completely invincible. Does NOT give players purple shards (unless you want it to) but it can give unobtained gun parts that were collected during any God Mode runs. Can also be used in custom mode when unlocked.

ridiculous Mode: A game mode where a ridiculous amount of hard enemies spawn, and the player only gets weak weapons. Also, the player doesn’t get shop rooms, armor rooms, question mark rooms, keys, shields, neutralize, deflect, upgrade rooms, health rooms, or max health rooms, and all of the rooms are challenge rooms. The player also starts with a max health of one, deals 50% less damage. The enemies have 200% more health, move 100% faster if they can move, have a 300% higher firerate, have the capacity of how many can exist at a time increased by 1000%, can destroy gun parts with their bullets, and spawn 500% faster. Also forces the player to do the secret zone after defeating the core. Unlocked when Nightmare Mode is beaten 5 times, and has a description of: “So you thought Nightmare Mode was easy? Well then, you tryhard, this is the place for you.”

Free Movement mode: Basically a regular game except for one thing: YOU CAN MOVE AROUND THE ARENA AND JUMP. Probably will use different maps if this is added. Not sure what to make this cost in purple shards. Could be added to custom mode if it gets added to the game. It’s ok if this doesn’t added to the game: I just thought it would be a cool idea.

Other-

Enemy Index: An index that shows all of the enemies that you’ve killed (including bosses) and their attacks.

Collection+: A unique feature unlocked at 1 million purple shards. Allows you to collect extra gun parts for your usage in sandbox mode. This means that weapons that have rarities other than their own that you collect save in sandbox mode, any extra gun parts you already have will save in sandbox mode, and basically any you collect during one of your runs will save in sandbox mode for you to build with. You also only get one of anything you collect. This feature can have certain parts of it toggled and reset in settings. Turning it to “No Collection” pauses collection, “Remove Extra” removes what you collected until turned on (any other parts you collect will be saved as well unless no collection is on as well), and “Reset Extras” deletes any extra pieces from your sandbox building menu.

If you are some random guy who read this, then you may look upon this freely. If you are any one of the devs of this game and you read this, I would really appreciate it if you added any of these things to the game. You don’t have to add all of these to the game, of course, but if you do add any of these, I won’t mind if you change anything around if you do decide to add these to the game, as long as you don’t change too much. I may update this from time to time if I get more ideas. Made by a guy who wants to share his imagination with the world. Also, I found a glitch where you can put a shield (not sure about anything else) on your left hand connected when you already have a connector (dice connector is the only one I tried) on your left hand. It didn’t seem to work on my right hand connector, and I also found it on total accident.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words--it's much appreciated!

reviews.review_title: I got to platinum, I want more!
reviews.review_description: Developers, guys, where is the announcement of Mothergunship 2? You have promoted quality gaming before, keep promoting it!

I will echo new content would be so awesome but I've had it for months now and I still play it daily.  I wish it wasn't so expensive as I would love to put it on my kid's headsets so we could play together. I know my 9 year old son would LOVE to play it. I highly recommend this game. It's very fun to play, however I do have one gripe. On sandbox mode even if all the parts have been acquired you have to have saved the gun at the end of the run for them to show up in your arsenal for sandbox. 
I would love to see some more customization for your avatar, and being able to play around a little more in your cockpit at the beginning would also be entertaining. All in all a GREAT game. You won't be disappointed!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the wonderful review! I'm glad that you think it's worthy of the 9 year old--they can be harsh judges for sure! I hope Forge continues to provide many hours of joy for you and thanks for supporting small developers like us!

reviews.review_description: The gun parts on this game can make some crazy weapons. There are plenty of enemies to test your weapon mods on.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the nice review!

reviews.review_title: One of the best co-op games on Quest
reviews.review_description:
Update: May 2025
Multiplayer works fine again. 
Still one of the best co-op games on Quest with the most replayability. 

Update: 28 Jan 2025
This game is normally 5 stars, and it’s the game I have played the most, but MULTI-PLAYER IS BROKEN YET AGAIN!!! Any friend invite simply results in “something went wrong” pop-up. Please fix this! Love this game! Ironically, multiplayer broke about this exact same time in Jan 2023 - coincidence?

Update: 13 Feb 2023
They fixed multiplayer pretty fast this time, so it was only down for about a week. It’s a great game, definitely in the top 3 multiplayer motion comfort friendly games on Quest. 

Update: 26 Jan 2023
They broke multiplayer again so we’re back to our 1 star review until they fix it. Network error 8. Nothing fixes the issue. 

UPDATE: 14 Dec 2022
Multiplayer was broken a few months ago, but updates to the game have fixed it.  Finally got to play this game with my friends, and it's a blast! (lol pun intended)
Quest2 desperately needs motion comfort-friendly games, and Mother Gunship: Forge fits the bill perfectly!
~ Love crafting & testing different weapon combos
~ Loads of different game modes for replayability
~ 2-player campaign for maximum social fun.

5 STARS, EASY!

PREVIOUS REVIEW (when multiplayer was broken):
Got this game to play multiplayer with friends. But the multiplayer mode via friend invite was completely broken and didn’t work. Whether I or my friend sent an invite, the person invited would click join, and their headset would go black for a few seconds, but then either end with a “Network 8 error”, or give a “host disconnected” message, even though we hadn’t disconnected. Just wouldn’t work. Real shame. Returning the game until they can get their multiplayer bugs fixed.

developer_response.body: Hm.. this seems to be related to something on the meta side, although we are not sure what. Feel free to hop on to our discord if you'd like to talk through it a bit more in detail with us (discord.terribleposture.com), since it can be difficult to diagnose things here in reviews.

reviews.review_description:
It's 
Been a while since any new content has been made. This game is one of a kind and one of the best. Pls continue making a new campaign new bosses keep this masterpiece going. So many followers with positive reviews don't stop it now.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're a very small team and there's only so many things we can do at once, but we're grateful that you and so many others have enjoyed our work!

reviews.review_description: I've had this game since pretty much the month it came out and I first remember when they had the duplication glitch it was so fun and amazing having it i just kept on duplicating stuff and having fun messing around it was a good time while it lasted also there hasn't been much updates I wish there were more and I also love the food items.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! And...yeah... that duplication glitch was wacky. We're glad you liked the food items too. Making all that stuff --but metal-- was a lot of fun for us. Thanks for the kind review. :-)

reviews.review_description: fun

developer_response.body: thank you!

reviews.review_description: This game is one of the best VR games I’ve ever played. The sheer customization and rougelike nature of the game already adds so much replay value, that I’ve had the game for a long time now and I’m still not done. Not to mention the relatively cheap price, especially compared to other games these days. I also can’t say that I’ve encountered any glitches or bugs since I’ve started playing. I can’t recommend this game enough, give it a try if you like vr shooters and rougelikes.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! This is the kind of review we love to see. We hope you have many more hours of enjoyment left in the game, too!

reviews.review_description: My friend and I both recently got a Quest 3 and have been playing this game together. Customizing your gun is pretty cool and the gameplay loop is very fun! Highly recommend for a 2 player co-op shooter! I just need to stop punching my desk.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! We're glad you are enjoying the game :-)

reviews.review_description:
Hey Devs!!! Any chance we could get a version of Tower of Guns to the Meta Quest?!?!?

I absolutely love and have played that game monthly since release lol

developer_response.body: Director Joe here: Oh, wow, you're an old school fan for sure. First, thanks for the support all these years--players like you are the reason we still remain excited to make games. Second, if you played Tower of Guns, then you likely know it was a mostly-solo project made by an artist-turned-programmer (me) and I definitely did not know the best ways to do things. The engine the game was made it was also quite old, even for the time. Bringing that particular game to quest is preetttty unlikely. I do think there's a lot of things in that game that would work well in VR though... like the Maw in particular! So, I share your enthusiasm for the idea!

reviews.review_description: Get it. Play it. I’m hooked. I would like to find a quick glossary (e.g. X2 critical to barrels?) on descriptions.

developer_response.body: Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Seriously, you build up your gun piece by piece. Want to launch Potatoes? Sure. Pizzas? 100%. Rockets? Go for it! 
I think it's a pioneer in the genre of VR Bullet hells!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We had a lot of fun making this thing. :-)

reviews.review_description: Gave a 5 star review a few months ago and kindly addressed a few issues with the app that I was hoping they could fix. I never received a response from the devs. They only seem to respond to 100% positive reviews. Very frustrating.

reviews.review_title: Has give me endless hours of entertainme
reviews.review_description: I love this game IV played for many hours and completed all the various game types and difficulty levels. I would have given it 5 stars if multiplayer had worked but it's never matched me with a single player and doesn't seem to work at all.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review. We haven't had too many reports like yours of no matching at all. While we can't promise we can fix everything, we do genuinely care about people's experiences and want to know more about what's going on. Can you send us an email at support (at) terribleposture (dot) com?

reviews.review_description:
After having fun for may be 3 hours. The game has finally stopped from loading. Every time I try to lunch the game the game isn’t responding.

I tried re-installing the game nothing is working.

reviews.review_title: Best 2 player wave game I’ve played
reviews.review_description: •The co-op gameplay is pristine and better than most games- •The sheer amount of customization you can have in this game is out of this world- •The gun mechanics are amazing- •The amount of guns,ability’s,ability upgrades and weapon upgrade give you a new experience every time- •The way you can chose your path gives the game a unique experience every play through.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We're happy you liked our work!

reviews.review_description:
Not too sure how old this game is. But the way it played seemed  like the early days of vr.

UPDATE: I gave this game a chance, and after playing some more, i realized it's an awesome game . The creation of some of the most hilarious and powerful guns can be extremely addictive 😄

developer_response.body: Hey there; we assume you are talking about the room-scale approach to combat feeling dated, which somewhat limits the movement of the player. There were a lot of reasons we made that call, but the biggest reason was that we are a super small team and knew we needed to focus on what made this game different, and not how it was similar to other shooters. Namely, the crafting variety and customization was our big focus. We hope you give the game another try with that in mind someday and we welcome further feedback!

reviews.review_description: Terribly disappointed! I had high hopes, but it’s extremely repetitive and gets boring very quickly. I gave two stars because of the detail of the weapons in your hand due to the high resolution. But that is definitely not a reason to buy this game.

developer_response.body: Ah, we're sorry you didn't enjoy the game. We definitely put our focus on the gun parts and wild crafting, and for some people the arena-style-progression just isn't their cup of tea. Thanks for trying it anyway. Perhaps someday you'll give it another go and find more things that you like about it, but either way, we appreciate the the feedback.

reviews.review_title: Having lots of Bugs! Data wiped.
reviews.review_description: Ok, so I had been playing this game for a few weeks, a friend of mine suggested it and it's fantastic, loads of fun and one of my favorite VR experiences period. But I recently ran into an issue where it reset my entire progress after my headset died as I was playing it. I was reset back to level 1, and after playing for a while over again, I went to surrender. But when I got to the defeat room where you can see all of your unlocks and the guns you played with, it never showed any screen and the guns never showed up, I was locked in the death room with no way out other than quitting the game altogether. Additionally, the in game announcer npcs would spam their quotes every... Single... Time that I found a powerup or a boss door or an upgrade tool. I don't know what happened to my game but it's impossible to progress now and I'm sad to have to stop playing my favorite VR game so far. If there's a way to restore this issue please let me know or make an update patch.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! First off, thanks for the review. But, dang, I'm sorry for those issues!
You're seeing some definitely unexpected things--we saw a few similar reports a couple weeks back, coinciding with an Oculus platform update--and it caused some ripple effects like what you are describing... so this could still be that issue... but you may be hitting something new too.
Either way, we want to help. If you see this, please send us an email to: support (at) terribleposture (dot) com and let's see if we can get you properly up and running again!  Please include WHEN you saw that first data loss issue and any other clues you may have.

reviews.review_description: Doesn’t live up to the hype it’s given. I’m an avid roguelike rogue lite player but this kinda doesn’t have that aspect of engrossed replay-ability.

developer_response.body:
Ouch. Harsh. We can understand the game isn't for everyone, but single-star reviews always sting! 
Still, we get that roguelites can definitely be the kinds of games a player either bounces off of or magically clicks with. We'd love to hear more in depth feedback from you if you care to share (support (at) terribleposture (dot) com) and, obviously, we hope that some day you'll come back and give the game a second look to see if it changes your opinion at all.

reviews.review_title: Had to uninstall and download again
reviews.review_description: Son loves it but it wouldn’t work at first. Had to uninstall and download again.

developer_response.body: Huh. Sorry it wouldn't work for you initially. Maybe something was messed up during the download. Glad it got working for you in the end. Please feel free to reach out to support (at) terribleposture (dot) com and let us know what steps we can take to get that last star from you!

reviews.review_title: This is how it's done folks
reviews.review_description:
Here's an example of a dev team who's on top of their game. The team clearly has such a great command over the technical bits that they can concentrate on the content and have fun. This team delivers!

I thought the sandbox mode would be a "sell-out" or fan service, but no, it turned out to be a challenging and legitimate game mode.

The game is solid. Now you'll just have to add more easter eggs and general nonsense :D

Oh, please add a bolter/massdriver barrel with fast heavy bullets that SLAP!

developer_response.body: Hot Dang, I'm gonna share this review with the team. This is such a nice comment. Thanks for playing and we're looking forward to adding as much nonsense as we can to this thing!

reviews.review_title: This is an insult to the original game
reviews.review_description: This watered-down VR version is nothing like the original. Instead running around a large room avoiding crazy hoards, you are standing in one place shooting in one general direction. No matter what anybody says, this game is not Mothergunship, this is a cash grab.

developer_response.body:
Wow, ouch. :-( 

So, first off, sorry you were so disappointed. I can definitely understand how you'd feel if the big thing you loved about the first game was the acrobatics. We're a super small team working on the VR game, and so we focused on the *other* core pillar of the first game, that crafting system. Rebuilding that system from the ground up for VR and basing all our other work around it became a sort of guiding pillar for us. It did result in a different sort of game, yes, but definitely not a cash grab; we feel like we built a pretty strong foundation here that's fun to play.

That said, I totally get why you are critical and don't blame you. We've been adding more into the game with updates and we are listening to you all and taking notes. One star reviews always sting; so perhaps someday in a few months, try us out again and see if you think we've earned another star or two from you?

reviews.review_description: This is the first rogue lite game I’ve ever been happy buying. MGF is in the top 5 must have Quest VR games for me. There are some bugs, but the devs are communicative and the community is solid. Nothing beats grabbing some guns with your partner and shooting a bunch of robotic enemies to relieve some stress. Plus, it’s super responsive gameplay and different weapon mods really make it a joy to play.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the nice review! If you want to relay any of those bugs you found, feel free to drop us a note in our discord at discord.terrible.posture.com

reviews.review_description: Really good to brun some time!

developer_response.body: Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Was looking forward to play with my frend in multiplayer but was disappointed to find a garbage wave battle was hopeing was the same as solo but with friends

developer_response.body: Hey there! It's been a long time since your review; we've since added a ton of multiplayer stuff, including the full campaign. I hope you get a chance to try the game again sometime.

reviews.review_description:
I was skeptical about this game but I have to say I love it the graphics looks great the gameplay is fantastic and creating a gun that looks like a monster from frankenstien is a beautiful thing, I am not normally a rouge lite person but this game is an absolute must have for anyone on the fence about it.

Only thing I would like is the ability to free walk with a bit more space and also would love more achievements but that's just a personal thing because I love achievements lol.

Get this game it's a must have and I can't wait to see what else comes to it in the future!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind review and the wishlist items there. We're taking notes!

reviews.review_description:
Mothergunship Forge in VR is an immersive and thrilling experience that takes the already great game to a whole new level. The VR technology enhances the game's fast-paced combat and crafting system, making players feel like they are truly in the middle of the action. The game's unique weapons and upgrade system is even more fun to use in VR, allowing players to physically interact with their creations. The graphics and level design look even more stunning in VR, and the game's boss battles are even more challenging and satisfying to play. Overall, Mothergunship Forge in VR is an absolute must-play for fans of the genre, offering an unparalleled level of immersion and excitement.

reviews.review_description: One of the big downsides is I've played with a couple people and they die and just leave right in the middle of the boss fight while I'm still going strong there goes another disconnect

You can always check out our discord (discord.terribleposture.com) where you can find more people to pair up with who are more dedicated players like yourself!

reviews.review_description: This game was amazing. The only thing is I wish is that the multiplayer was more like a regular mission an fighting bosses instead of kinda making it a competition.

developer_response.body: Thanks! We're reading all these reviews and taking notes on player feedback. We look forward to adding more to the game. :)

The gun building mechanic is very good
